about.desc: A browser-based multipurpose control + CAD/CAM workbench for hobby/desktop CNC machines (GRBL, grblHAL, FluidNC, Marlin and more) — CNC carving, engraving, pen-plotting, auto-soldering, PCB isolation routing, laser cutting and welding.
about.title: About karmyogi
ai.auth.sessionTip: Paste a logged-in session cookie via your own relay (advanced)
ai.chat.empty: Describe what you want to make. Then keep chatting to refine it — e.g. “make it 2mm deeper”, “add a 5mm border”, “use feed 300”. The whole conversation is sent as context and cached in this browser.
ai.chat.label: Chat
ai.copyTip: Copy the G-code to the clipboard
ai.creds.clearSessionTip: Remove the stored {site} cookie from this browser
ai.creds.clearTip: Remove the stored {provider} key from this browser
ai.err.noKey: Add your {provider} API key above first — the app needs it to call the model.
ai.err.noPrompt: Describe what you want to make first.
ai.err.noSession: Paste your {site} cookie above first, or switch to API-key mode.
ai.examplePrompt: Engrave the text "HELLO" 20mm tall centered on the bed, 0.3mm deep, single-stroke. Use a 1mm engraving bit, feed 400 mm/min, plunge 150 mm/min.
ai.intro: Describe a job in plain words and the model writes GRBL G-code for it. This runs entirely in your browser using your own API key (or a pasted login session) — there is no server.
ai.key.label: {provider} API key
ai.key.note: Your key stays in this browser (localStorage) and is sent only to {provider}.
ai.loadTip: Load this G-code into the Program tab so it shows in the Visualizer for review
ai.loadedNote: Loaded into Program — open the Visualizer to review the toolpath before cutting.
ai.model.label: Model
ai.safety: AI-generated G-code can be wrong or unsafe. ALWAYS review it in the Visualizer and dry-run (Z raised / spindle off) before cutting. Never run it unattended.
ai.session.cookiePlaceholder: paste the cookie you copied (header string or JSON both work)
ai.session.importFail: Could not read a cookie from {name}. Use a cookies.txt, JSON export, or paste it manually.
ai.session.importTip: Import a cookie file you saved from a cookie extension (.txt or .json)
ai.session.label: {site} login session
ai.session.openTip: Open {site} in a new tab and log in
ai.session.simple: Already logged in to {site}? Copy your cookie with a cookie extension (e.g. Cookie-Editor) and paste it below — no API key needed.
ai.session.stored: Your cookie stays in this browser (localStorage). If the session expires, paste a fresh one.
ai.setup.title: Model & context — {model} · bed {w}×{d} mm
app.subtitle: CAD/CAM workbench
cam.bt.auto.abortTip: Stop the calibration and cancel the jog
cam.bt.auto.btnTip: Jog a grid, auto-detect the tool, and solve the calibration
cam.bt.auto.confirm: The machine will jog to {n} points around the current position (±{s} mm in X/Y, Z untouched). Make sure the tool is clear and raised. Continue?
cam.bt.auto.guide: Aim a camera at the bed so the tool is visible in the feed above and raise the tool clear of the work. Then press Auto-calibrate: the machine jogs to a small XY grid around the current position, snaps a frame at each, and finds the tool automatically (no clicking).
cam.bt.auto.notConnected: Machine not connected — connect it in the Controller tab first.
cam.bt.auto.notConnectedHint: Machine not connected. Connect in the Controller tab — auto-calibration jogs the tool to a grid of known points.
cam.bt.err.degenerate: Could not solve — points may be collinear.
cam.bt.err.noFrame: No live frame — start this camera first.
cam.bt.err.noRegistry: Marker registry unavailable — use manual or machine motion.
cam.bt.feed.autoNote: Live frames stream to the server automatically — no clicking needed.
cam.bt.feed.failed: Send failed — is a camera live?
cam.bt.feed.name: name (e.g. rest, x20)
cam.bt.feed.nameTip: Optional: save a one-off labelled frame as .camera-frames/<name>.png
cam.bt.feed.sendTip: Save a one-off labelled frame (the live feed already streams automatically)
cam.bt.feed.startNote: Start a camera above (allow the permission) and frames stream to the server automatically.
cam.bt.feed.testTip: Start a synthetic in-app camera (no webcam / no permission) and stream it to the server — for testing the whole pipeline
cam.bt.feed.title: Camera → server bridge
cam.bt.hint: Teach a camera where the bed is so the 3D viewer can show the real machine behind your toolpaths and check whether the design fits the stock.
cam.bt.hull.badH: Calibration matrix is not invertible — recalibrate.
cam.bt.hull.missing: Both slots need calibration + an empty-bed reference + a live frame.
cam.bt.hull.need: Needs both cameras calibrated + live with empty-bed references
cam.bt.hull.needTwo: Two calibrated views are required for height.
cam.bt.hull.noPose: Could not derive camera pose — recalibrate.
cam.bt.hull.sizeMismatch: Reference and live frame sizes differ — re-capture the reference.
cam.bt.hull.tip: Estimate stock height by two-camera shape-from-silhouette
cam.bt.job.badSize: Enter a positive width and depth.
cam.bt.job.detectTip: Detect the workpiece from STOCK QR stickers (needs Cam 1 calibrated)
cam.bt.job.needCalib: Calibrate slot 1 first so stock pixels map to mm.
cam.bt.job.noStock: No STOCK markers detected on the workpiece.
cam.bt.job.set: Job set to {w}×{d}×{t} mm at bed center.
cam.bt.job.setTip: Place a job of this size at the bed center
cam.bt.jobHint: Tell karmyogi where the workpiece sits so it can fit-check the design. Detect it from STOCK stickers, or set the size by hand.
cam.bt.m.autoTip: Recommended — the machine jogs to a grid and auto-detects the tool
cam.bt.m.machineTip: Fallback — jog the tool to known XY and click it in the frame
cam.bt.m.manualTip: Offline fallback — click the 4 bed corners
cam.bt.m.qrTip: Read the printed TARGET QR codes
cam.bt.manual.guide: Click the 4 bed corners in the frame above in this order: top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left. World corners come from the bed size ({w}×{d} mm).
cam.bt.manual.solveTip: Solve the homography from the 4 clicked bed corners
cam.bt.mm.addFirst: Press 'Add point' first to capture machine X/Y.
cam.bt.mm.addTip: Record the current machine work X/Y, then click the tool tip
cam.bt.mm.guide: Aim a camera at the bed so the machine’s tool is visible in the feed above. Then, for ≥4 spread-out spots: jog the tool there (Controller jog pad), press “Add point”, and click the tool’s tip in the image. Then “Solve”.
cam.bt.mm.notConnected: Machine not connected. Connect in the Controller tab to use machine-motion calibration — it reads live work X/Y as you jog.
cam.bt.mm.pending: ↳ Now click your tool’s TIP in the camera image above — the very end of the bit / pen / nozzle, where it touches near the bed. That pairs that pixel with the machine position X{x} Y{y} you just recorded. Then jog elsewhere and repeat.
cam.bt.mm.solveTip: Solve the camera↔machine homography from the collected pairs
cam.bt.mm.spread: Points are clustered — spread them across the bed for a stable solve.
cam.bt.qr.guide: Lay the printed sheet flat on the bed with all 4 TARGET codes visible, then scan.
cam.bt.qr.notEnough: Found {n}/4 TARGET markers — need all 4 visible.
cam.bt.qr.scanTip: Scan the frame for the 4 TARGET QR codes and solve
cam.bt.qr.unsupported: This browser has no BarcodeDetector. Use machine motion or bed-corner calibration instead.
cam.bt.ref.tip: Store an empty-bed reference frame for this camera (used by height estimation)
cam.bt.showAria: Show the live camera overlay in the 3D viewport
cam.bt.title: Bed tracking (3D)
cam.calib.downloadTip: Download the printable A4 calibration sheet (PDF)
cam.calib.hint: Print this QR fiducial sheet at 100% scale and lay it flat on the bed. The camera reads the codes to learn mm-per-pixel + perspective, then measures bed and stock size automatically. Cut out the S# stickers for the workpiece.
cam.calib.openTip: Open the calibration sheet in a new tab to print
cam.calib.title: Calibration sheet
cam.camera.title: Camera
cam.capture.recordTip: Start recording the live feed to a WebM clip
cam.capture.snapshotTip: Capture the current frame as a PNG and download it
cam.capture.stopRecTip: Stop recording and download the clip
cam.capture.title: Capture
cam.clips.empty: Recordings and timelapses you save show up here.
cam.clips.remove: Remove from this list (does not delete a downloaded file)
cam.clips.title: Clips
cam.empty.denied.body: Camera access was blocked. Allow it in the address-bar site permissions, then press the power button again. (getUserMedia needs HTTPS or localhost.)
cam.empty.denied.title: Permission denied
cam.empty.error.title: Camera error
cam.empty.nocamera.body: No video input device is available. Plug in a webcam and it will appear here.
cam.empty.nocamera.title: No camera found
cam.empty.off.body: Press the power button to start the live feed. getUserMedia needs HTTPS or localhost.
cam.empty.off.title: Camera off
cam.empty.starting.body: Grant permission if your browser asks.
cam.empty.starting.title: Starting camera…
cam.empty.unsupported.body: This browser has no getUserMedia. Use a Chromium browser over HTTPS or localhost.
cam.empty.unsupported.title: Camera not supported
cam.feed.title: Feed
cam.intro: Live webcam feed — record clips, grab snapshots, capture a timelapse, and calibrate the camera to the 3D bed so you can preview the real machine. Everything stays in your browser.
cam.slot2.hint: A second camera (different angle) lets karmyogi estimate the stock HEIGHT by shape-from-silhouette. Optional — leave off for a single top-down view.
cam.slot2.title: Second camera
cam.timelapse.fpsSub: frames per second in the output video
cam.timelapse.hint: Grab a frame every interval, play them back fast into one webm.
cam.timelapse.stopTip: Stop the timelapse, assemble the webm and download it
cam.timelapse.title: Timelapse
cc.adv2dTip: Feed rates, decimals & line numbers — defaults are usually fine
cc.advAutoTip: Auto-computed speeds & depths from your bit + material. Plunge-Z is editable; the rest are derived for you.
cc.advTip: Feed rates and spindle speed — defaults are usually fine
cc.applyAll: Apply this setting to all jobs
cc.autoBadgeTip: Auto-set from your bit + material — editable
cc.autoFootnote: These update automatically when you change the bit or material. Only Plunge-Z and Safe-Z are yours to tweak.
cc.ballTip: Ball-nose: best for smooth relief finishing
cc.bedHint: Bed {w}×{d}mm — jobs auto-nest with a {m}mm gap.
cc.bitLiveHint: feeds drive the safe-passes suggestion below.
cc.breakThroughTip: Extra depth below the stock bottom so the cut goes fully through
cc.carvingOffThread: Carving off the main thread — the UI stays responsive
cc.cdrUnsupported: CorelDRAW .cdr is a proprietary binary format with no reliable in-browser parser. In CorelDRAW choose File → Export → DXF (or SVG/EPS) and import that — karmyogi fully supports DXF, including splines & ellipses.
cc.closedTip: Closed loops — needed for Profile / Pocket
cc.connectFirst: Connect to a machine to send
cc.cutSpeedTip: Cutting feed while the tool is engaged in material
cc.cutStepdownTip: Depth removed per profile pass through the stock
cc.cutoutClear: Clear material around part (pocket to bottom)
cc.cutoutClearTip: Instead of only cutting the perimeter, clear all the stock between the cut boundary and the part down to the bottom level — leaving the part standing on a cleared floor.
cc.cutoutHint: Cuts through each job’s own stock thickness, riding outside the part edge. Set 0 tabs only if the part is held some other way.
cc.cutoutHintRect: Cuts a rectangle through each job’s own stock thickness. Set 0 tabs only if the part is held some other way.
cc.cutoutMarginTip: Extra space left around the part’s bounding box on every side
cc.cutoutOutlineTip: Cut along the carved part’s outer edge — the tool rides just outside the part outline.
cc.cutoutRectAutoTip: Size the rectangle to the part bounding box plus a margin
cc.cutoutRectManualTip: Set an explicit origin and size for the rectangle
cc.cutoutRectTip: Cut a rectangle you size yourself — auto-fit to the part plus a margin, or set explicit X/Y origin and size.
cc.cutoutRectXTip: Lower-left X of the rectangle in bed coordinates
cc.cutoutRectYTip: Lower-left Y of the rectangle in bed coordinates
cc.cutoutTip: After the relief is carved, cut the part free from the block. Choose to follow the part’s outline or cut a rectangle, optionally clearing the material around the part. Holding tabs leave small bridges so the part stays put until you snap it out.
cc.decimalsTip: Number of decimal places in emitted coordinates
cc.depthOverStock: Cut depth ({d}mm) is deeper than the stock ({h}mm) — the tool may hit the bed.
cc.dropHint: or drop a .stl / .step / .dxf / .eps / .ai file anywhere on this panel
cc.dropHintMulti: or drop a .stl / .dxf / .eps / .ai file anywhere — each STL adds a job
cc.engraveHelp: Follow every line at one depth — good for V-carving text & detail.
cc.errEps: Couldn’t parse this EPS/AI — export as DXF.
cc.errNoTriangles: STL parsed but contained no triangles.
cc.exportStl: Export your STEP model as STL to carve it.
cc.finishAllowanceTip: Extra clearance beyond the tool radius left on the part edge
cc.finishing: Finishing — parallel raster following the relief surface
cc.freeSpeedTip: Travel feed for non-cutting links between one cut and the next
cc.importTipMulti: Add an .stl model as a job (import again for more) — or a .dxf / .eps / .ai vector file
cc.importToGen: Import a model to generate a toolpath.
cc.intro: Import an STL to carve in relief, or a DXF / vector file for 2D engrave · profile · pocket — the toolpath previews live in the Visualizer, then press Send.
cc.introMulti: Import one or more STL models — each becomes a job that auto-nests on the bed and carves in a single combined program. DXF / vector files do 2D engrave · profile · pocket.
cc.jobEnable: Include this job in the program
cc.jobSelect: Select to edit this job’s settings
cc.load.bad: Could not load — not a valid carving settings file.
cc.matInfoHint: Baseline feeds/speeds for a hobby 3-axis router — scaled for your actual bit on the Recommended panel.
cc.maxDepthHint: Carve depth limits how far below the model’s top surface the tool goes (protects the bed / clamps).
cc.needClosed: {op} needs a closed contour — none found in this file.
cc.noJobs: No models yet — Add a model above. Import again to nest more side-by-side.
cc.notConnected: Not connected — preview is live; connect to send.
cc.openProgramTab: Generated live — open the Program tab to stream, pause or step it.
cc.penHint: Pen: cuts map to pen-down Z, travels to pen-up Z (no spindle).
cc.penModeTip: Pen plotter: cuts → pen-down Z, travels → pen-up Z (no spindle)
cc.placementJobHint: X/Y/rotation/scale move just THIS job. Re-nest packs all jobs without overlap.
cc.pocketHelp: Clear out the inside area of closed shapes, pass by pass.
cc.primaryHint: One bit cuts every job. Speeds & depth are worked out from these — see Advanced (auto) below.
cc.profileHelp: Cut along closed shapes (on / inside / outside the line).
cc.recIntro: Safe passes worked out for {mat} with a {bit} — let karmyogi decide for you.
cc.renest: Re-nest all jobs on the bed (no overlap)
cc.roughing: Roughing — clear bulk stock in flat stepdown layers
cc.sendTip: Stream this program to the machine
cc.spindleHint: Spindle: Z values are written verbatim; M3/M5 wrap the program.
cc.spindleModeTip: Router/spindle: Z is cut depth; M3/M5 control the spindle
cc.stepUnsupported: STEP import isn’t supported yet — full ISO-10303 (B-rep) parsing is out of scope. Please export your model as STL (most CAD tools: File → Export → STL) and import that instead.
cc.stepoverFracTip: Sideways overlap between pocket passes, as a fraction of tool ⌀
cc.stepoverHint3d: Smaller stepover = finer surface but more passes. Ball-nose is the classic relief finishing tool.
cc.stockLiveHint: The block shows live in the 3D Visualizer — look there to check fit.
cc.surfaceZTip: Z of the stock top — cuts go from here down to Cut depth
cc.tabCountTip: Number of bridges spaced evenly around the part
cc.tabHeightTip: Material left under each tab, measured up from the stock bottom
cc.tabLengthTip: Width of each tab along the perimeter
cc.tipBitLength: The usable cutting length of the bit — the deepest it can reach. Keep your total carve depth under this so the bit shank never rubs the stock.
cc.tipBitType: The shape of your cutter. Flat = straight bottom (pockets/profiles); Ball = rounded tip (smooth 3D relief). This picks the right tool model for the carve.
cc.tipBitWidth: The cutting diameter of your bit. A wider bit clears faster but loses fine detail; a narrower bit is slower but finer. All speeds/depths below are sized for this width.
cc.tipCuttingSpeed: How fast the bit moves while cutting — auto-derived from the material + bit so it cuts cleanly without stalling. Override if you know better.
cc.tipDepthPass: How much material each downward pass removes — kept shallow enough for your bit + material to stay safe. Lower it for hard material.
cc.tipFreeSpeed: Speed for non-cutting moves between cuts. Defaults to the machine maximum (rapid); lower it if rapids are too aggressive.
cc.tipMaterial: What you are cutting. Together with the bit, this decides safe feeds, spindle speed and depth-of-cut. Click a swatch to pick; click its picture for full details.
cc.tipPlungeZ: How fast the bit drives straight DOWN into the stock. Auto-set to a safe fraction of the cutting speed — lower it if your bit chatters when entering the cut.
cc.tipPullUp: How fast the bit retracts out of the cut. 0 = maximum (rapid G0). Set a value (mm/min) to lift more gently.
cc.tipSafeZ: Height the bit lifts to before moving across the stock. Must clear any clamps and the tallest part of your model.
cc.tipSpindle: Spindle speed suggested for this material — slower for plastics/metal, faster for wood. Override for your bit/spindle.
cc.tipStepover: Sideways spacing between cutting lines — finer means smoother but slower.
cc.title: 3D Carving.
cc.useRecTip: Copy these safe feeds & passes into the cut settings
cc.xyCenterTip: X0/Y0 at the centre of the stock
cc.xyFrontLeftTip: X0/Y0 at the front-left corner of the stock
cc.zBottomTip: Z0 at the bottom face of the stock
cc.zTopTip: Z0 at the top face of the stock (cut downwards)
conn.bridge.title: Server bridge: relay this browser’s machine to the karmyogi dev server so it can read state and send commands
conn.connect.title: Connect to the controller over USB (Web Serial)
conn.connect.unsupported: {name} can’t be driven live from a browser ({notes}). Use Mock to explore the UI, or generate G-code here and run it on the device.
conn.controller.label: Controller firmware
conn.controller.title: Select controller firmware
conn.machine.connectThis: Switch to and connect this machine
conn.machine.empty: No machines yet. Connect, or add one below.
conn.machine.label: Label (optional)
conn.machine.switch: Machines — switch the active machine or add one
conn.machine.wsHint: ESP3D / grblHAL-over-WebSocket / a serial↔ws bridge. Telnet needs a ws↔telnet bridge.
conn.mock.title: Connect to an in-browser mock GRBL device (no hardware)
console.clear.title: Clear the console log
console.empty.none: No messages yet — connect and send a command.
console.note.before: Raw GRBL console — type any G-code or
console.send.title: Send command (Enter)
coord.quick.allTitle: Set the current position as work zero for X, Y and Z (G10 L20 P0)
coord.quick.heading: Quick Zero
coord.quick.xyTitle: Set the current position as work zero for X and Y only (G10 L20 P0)
coord.wco.heading: Work Offset (WCO)
coord.wcs.heading: Work Coordinate System
ctrl.explain.feedOverride.body: A live dial to speed up or slow down the running job without editing it, shown as a percent of the programmed feed. Turn it down if the cut sounds harsh or struggles; 100% runs at the planned speed. Safe to adjust mid-cut.
ctrl.explain.feedOverride.title: Feed override
ctrl.explain.feedRate.body: The speed the tool moves through the material while cutting, in mm per minute. Higher is faster but harder on the bit; lower is slower and cleaner. Start conservative and increase only if the cut stays smooth.
ctrl.explain.feedRate.title: Feed rate
ctrl.explain.jogStep.body: How far the machine moves each time you tap a jog (arrow) button — for example 0.1, 1, or 10 mm. Big steps move quickly across the table; small steps let you nudge precisely. Use small steps near the workpiece.
ctrl.explain.jogStep.title: Jog step
ctrl.explain.rapidOverride.body: A live control for how fast the NON-cutting (travel) moves go, as a percent of full speed. Lower it (25% or 50%) when testing a new job so fast moves are easy to watch and stop. 100% is full travel speed.
ctrl.explain.rapidOverride.title: Rapid override
ctrl.explain.spindleOverride.body: A live dial to raise or lower the spinning speed while the job runs, as a percent of the programmed RPM. Nudge it down if the material burns, up if the bit bogs down. 100% runs at the planned speed.
ctrl.explain.spindleOverride.title: Spindle override
ctrl.explain.spindleRpm.body: How fast the cutting tool spins, in turns per minute. Higher speeds suit small bits and soft material; too fast can burn wood or melt plastic, too slow can chip the bit. Follow the bit/material chart, or start moderate.
ctrl.explain.spindleRpm.title: Spindle speed (RPM)
ctrl.hold.title: Feed hold — pause motion (!)
ctrl.home.title: Home — run the homing cycle ($H)
ctrl.jogfeed.title: Jog feed rate (mm/min) — speed of jog moves
ctrl.kbd.hint: Fully keyboard-operable when focused: arrows jog XY · PgUp/PgDn jog Z · Esc cancels · 1–4 step size · h Home · u Unlock · r Reset · ! Hold · ~ Resume · s Spindle · [ ] feed ∓ · \ feed 100%
ctrl.ov.feed.minus.title: Feed override −10% (key [)
ctrl.ov.feed.plus.title: Feed override +10% (key ])
ctrl.ov.feed.reset.title: Feed override reset to 100% (key \)
ctrl.ov.rapid.100.title: Rapid override 100% (full speed)
ctrl.ov.rapid.25.title: Rapid override 25%
ctrl.ov.rapid.50.title: Rapid override 50%
ctrl.ov.spindle.minus.title: Spindle override −10%
ctrl.ov.spindle.plus.title: Spindle override +10%
ctrl.ov.spindle.reset.title: Spindle override reset to 100%
ctrl.reset.title: Soft reset — abort & reset GRBL (Ctrl-X / 0x18)
ctrl.resume.title: Cycle resume — continue (~)
ctrl.speed.title: Spindle speed in RPM (S word sent with M3/M4)
ctrl.spindle.ccw.title: Counter-clockwise direction (M4)
ctrl.spindle.cw.title: Clockwise direction (M3)
ctrl.spindle.off.title: Spindle is OFF — click to start ({cmd}) · toggle with s
ctrl.spindle.on.title: Spindle is ON — click to stop (M5) · toggle with s
ctrl.unlock.title: Unlock — clear alarm / kill alarm lock ($X)
ctrl.zero.title: Zero — set the current position as work zero for X, Y and Z (G10 L20 P0)
explain.bit.body: The cutting tool fitted in the spindle, described by its type and width. The job uses it to size cuts and plan passes, so it must match what is actually installed. Swap the setting whenever you change the physical bit.
explain.bit.title: Bit
explain.cutDepthPerPass.body: How much material is removed in a single downward pass. Taking less per pass is gentler on the bit and gives a cleaner cut, but needs more passes. A common safe starting point is about half the bit width or less.
explain.cutDepthPerPass.title: Depth per pass
explain.cutSpeed.body: How fast the bit travels sideways through the material while cutting. Faster finishes sooner but strains the bit and motor; slower is cleaner and safer. Start slow on a new material and speed up if it cuts smoothly.
explain.cutSpeed.title: Cutting speed (feed rate)
explain.feedOverride.body: A live dial to speed up or slow down the running job without editing it, shown as a percent of the programmed feed. Turn it down if the cut sounds harsh or struggles; 100% runs at the planned speed. Safe to adjust mid-cut.
explain.feedOverride.title: Feed override
explain.feedRate.body: The speed the tool moves through the material while cutting, in mm per minute. Higher is faster but harder on the bit; lower is slower and cleaner. Start conservative and increase only if the cut stays smooth.
explain.feedRate.title: Feed rate
explain.finishing.body: A final light pass that shaves off the last thin layer for a smooth, accurate surface. It removes very little, so it is gentle and precise. Use it after roughing when surface quality matters.
explain.finishing.title: Finishing pass
explain.freeSpeed.body: How fast the machine moves when it is NOT cutting — repositioning in the air at safe height. This can be quite fast since nothing is being cut. Lower it if fast moves feel jerky or the machine loses position.
explain.freeSpeed.title: Travel speed (rapid)
explain.jogStep.body: How far the machine moves each time you tap a jog (arrow) button — for example 0.1, 1, or 10 mm. Big steps move quickly across the table; small steps let you nudge precisely. Use small steps near the workpiece.
explain.jogStep.title: Jog step
explain.material.body: What you are cutting — such as wood, plastic, aluminium, or PCB. It guides sensible speeds and depths, since each material cuts differently. Choosing the right one helps avoid burning, melting, or breaking the bit.
explain.material.title: Material
explain.plungeFeed.body: How fast the bit drives straight DOWN into the material before each cut. Going down is harder than cutting sideways, so this is slower than the cutting speed. Too fast can snap the bit; keep it gentle.
explain.plungeFeed.title: Plunge speed
explain.probeDistance.body: The furthest the tool will travel down looking for the plate before giving up and stopping with an alarm. It is a safety limit so the tool does not keep pushing if it never makes contact. Set it a little more than the expected gap.
explain.probeDistance.title: Probe max distance
explain.probeFeed.body: How fast the tool lowers toward the touch plate while finding the surface. Slow is more accurate and safer, since it stops the instant it touches. Keep it slow — there is no need to rush a probe.
explain.probeFeed.title: Probe speed
explain.rapidOverride.body: A live control for how fast the NON-cutting (travel) moves go, as a percent of full speed. Lower it (25% or 50%) when testing a new job so fast moves are easy to watch and stop. 100% is full travel speed.
explain.rapidOverride.title: Rapid override
explain.roughing.body: A fast first pass that clears most of the waste material, leaving a little extra behind. It does the heavy lifting quickly without worrying about a perfect finish. Follow it with a finishing pass for a clean surface.
explain.roughing.title: Roughing pass
explain.safeZ.body: The height the bit lifts to before moving sideways, so it clears the workpiece and clamps. Bigger = safer but a little slower; too small risks the bit crashing into the work or clamps. A few mm above the top is usually safe.
explain.safeZ.title: Safe height (Safe-Z)
explain.spindleOverride.body: A live dial to raise or lower the spinning speed while the job runs, as a percent of the programmed RPM. Nudge it down if the material burns, up if the bit bogs down. 100% runs at the planned speed.
explain.spindleOverride.title: Spindle override
explain.spindleRpm.body: How fast the cutting tool spins, in turns per minute. Higher speeds suit small bits and soft material; too fast can burn wood or melt plastic, too slow can chip the bit. Follow the bit/material chart, or start moderate.
explain.spindleRpm.title: Spindle speed (RPM)
explain.stepover.body: How far the bit shifts sideways between neighbouring passes when clearing an area. Smaller steps leave a smoother surface but take longer; larger steps are faster but rougher. Around 40–50% of the bit width is a good balance.
explain.stepover.title: Stepover
explain.toolDiameter.body: The width of the cutting bit. The machine uses it to keep cuts the right size and to space out passes, so it must match the bit actually fitted. Measure the bit if you are unsure — a wrong value makes parts the wrong size.
explain.toolDiameter.title: Tool diameter
explain.toolType.body: What kind of bit is fitted — flat end mill, V-bit, ball-nose, drill, or pen. It changes the shape the tool cuts and how toolpaths are calculated. Pick the one that matches the bit in the spindle.
explain.toolType.title: Tool type
explain.totalDepth.body: How deep the final cut goes overall, reached in several passes. Set it to your material thickness to cut through, or less to carve a groove. Going deeper than the material will cut into whatever is underneath.
explain.totalDepth.title: Total cut depth
explain.workZero.body: The spot on your material that counts as X0 Y0 Z0 — where the job is measured from. Usually a corner or the centre of the stock, with Z0 at the top surface. Set this before cutting so the job lands where you expect.
explain.workZero.title: Work zero (origin)
glue.adv.title: Plunge feed, dwell times and G-code decimals
glue.clear.title: Remove all shapes
glue.edit.label: Edit · {kind} (mm)
glue.field.dispenseRate.title: Dispenser output rate (drives the spindle/feeder S-word)
glue.field.dispenseZ.title: Z height (mm) at which the dispenser touches down to lay a bead
glue.field.feed.title: Trace feed rate (mm/min) while dispensing along an outline
glue.field.travelZ.title: Safe Z height (mm) for rapid travel between shapes
glue.hint.box: Drag a bounding box on the bed
glue.hint.circle: Drag from the centre outwards to set the radius
glue.hint.line: Drag from one end of the bead to the other
glue.hint.select: Drag a shape to move it · Delete removes the selected one
glue.intro.post: The G-code updates live in the Program tab — run it from there.
glue.intro.pre: Pick a shape and draw it on the bed.

glue.list.pick.title: Select this shape

glue.raw.title: Show the generated G-code text

laser.cut.decimals.title: Coordinate precision in the emitted G-code.
laser.cut.passes.title: How many times each contour is cut.
laser.cut.power.title: Laser power as an S value (0..{sMax} = 0..100%). Currently {pct}%.
laser.cut.powerPct.title: Laser power as a percentage of S-max.
laser.cut.sMax.title: Max S value the controller maps to 100% (GRBL $30).
laser.cut.speed.title: Feed rate while cutting (G1 F…).
laser.cut.title: Cut parameters
laser.dxf.hint: Import a DXF: closed contours become cut loops, open paths become cut lines.
laser.dxf.title: Drawing (DXF)
laser.focus.hint: Z is left untouched — focus the head manually.
laser.focus.note.co2: CO2 focus is usually fixed/manual; set a Z here only if your machine focuses by Z.
laser.focus.note.fiber: Note: true capacitive height-following is not possible in plain GRBL — this sets a fixed focus Z.
laser.focus.z.title: Absolute Z moved to at program start to set focus.
laser.load.bad: Could not load — not a valid laser settings file.
laser.nest.fit: {fit} of {total} copies fit on the {w}×{h} mm sheet
laser.pierce.hint.co2: CO2 normally starts the cut immediately (no pierce). Enable only for thick material.
laser.pierce.hint.fiber: Fiber cuts normally pierce before each contour. Enable to dwell at the start point.
laser.pierce.power.title: Beam power during the pre-cut pierce dwell (typically higher than cut power).
laser.pierce.time.title: Dwell at the contour start before cutting begins (G4 P…).
laser.powerMode.title: M4 dynamic scales power with feed (best for cutting); M3 is constant power.
laser.safety: Safety: laser OFF (M5 S0) during all travel; beam on (M3/M4 S…) only on cut feeds; requires GRBL laser mode $32=1.
laser.sheet.h.title: Usable sheet height (Y).
laser.sheet.qty.title: Number of copies of the imported part to lay out.
laser.sheet.spacing.title: Gap kept between parts and around the sheet edge.
laser.sheet.title: Sheet & nesting
laser.sheet.w.title: Usable sheet width (X).
laser.status.syncTitle: Lines auto-synced to the Program tab
laser.title: Laser Cutting
launch.label: Open / reopen panels
launch.title: Panels
motion.alert.corruptPlural: {count} settings look corrupted. Review the highlighted rows below, or factory-reset to recover.
motion.alert.corruptSingular: 1 setting looks corrupted. Review the highlighted rows below, or factory-reset to recover.
motion.cap.heading: Capabilities
motion.confirm.clearOffsets:
Clear all work-coordinate offsets (G54–G59, G28/G30, G92)?

Machine settings are kept; only your zero offsets are erased.
motion.confirm.resetSettings:
Restore the machine to its known-good default configuration?

This runs $RST=$ then writes the karmyogi default profile (steps/mm 1600, max rate 1000, accel 30, max travel 200, etc.) — use it to recover a corrupted or mis-configured controller.
motion.confirm.wipeAll:
FULL EEPROM WIPE: reset BOTH settings AND coordinate offsets to defaults?

This is the nuclear option — everything goes back to factory.
motion.discard.label: Discard
motion.discard.title: Discard pending edits
motion.factory.clearOffsets.label: Clear offsets ($RST=#)
motion.factory.clearOffsets.title: $RST=# — clear G54–G59 coordinate offsets
motion.factory.heading: Factory reset
motion.factory.note: Use these to recover from corrupted EEPROM. Each asks for confirmation, then re-reads settings.
motion.factory.resetSettings.label: Reset settings ($RST=$)
motion.factory.resetSettings.title: $RST=$ — restore settings ($0–$132) to defaults
motion.factory.wipeAll.label: Wipe all ($RST=*)
motion.factory.wipeAll.title: $RST=* — full EEPROM wipe (settings + offsets)
motion.marlin.consoleHint: Reported values stream into the Console panel. Set a value by typing the M-code there, e.g. M92 X80 Y80 Z400, then M500 to persist.
motion.marlin.notice: Marlin stores settings in EEPROM and manages them via M-codes — not GRBL `$`-settings. Use M503 to report the current values, M500 to save, and M92 / M203 / M201 / M204 / M205 to set steps, feedrates, and acceleration.
motion.marlin.ref.heading: M-code reference
motion.marlin.report.label: ⤓ Report settings (M503)
motion.marlin.report.title: Send M503 — report current settings into the Console
motion.marlin.save.label: 💾 Save to EEPROM (M500)
motion.marlin.save.title: Send M500 — save current settings to EEPROM
motion.marlin.sendCode.title: Send {code} to the controller
motion.masso.notice: {label} is a standalone controller — all motion and machine settings are configured on its own touchscreen, not from the host. It also has no GRBL-style host-streaming serial protocol, so karmyogi can’t connect live or read/write settings.
motion.masso.workflow.body: Use karmyogi as a CAD/CAM + G-code generator: design or import your job, generate safe G-code, then copy the .nc/.gcode file to a USB stick and run it from the Masso pendant. Configure feeds, homing, soft limits, spindle and probing on the Masso touchscreen itself.
motion.masso.workflow.heading: Offline / export workflow
motion.none.notice: {label} doesn't expose editable machine settings over this connection — configure motion / laser parameters in the controller's own software.
motion.note.accelPost: (trapezoidal ramps, no S-curve / jerk control). $120–$122 are constant accel in mm/sec².
motion.note.connect: Connect to a GRBL device to read or edit settings.
motion.note.connectDefaults: Showing default GRBL settings below. Connect to a GRBL device and press Sync to read and edit the live values from your machine.
motion.notes.heading: About this controller
motion.pending.title: Edited — click Save changes
motion.reset.title: Reset ${num} to default ({value}) — then Save to apply
motion.save.label: 💾 Save changes
motion.save.title: Save all edited parameters to the machine
motion.smoothie.consoleHint: Values stream into the Console panel. To change one, type e.g. config-set sd alpha_steps_per_mm 80 there, then reset the board to apply.
motion.smoothie.get.label: ⤓ config-get sd alpha_steps_per_mm
motion.smoothie.get.title: Send config-get sd alpha_steps_per_mm — value streams into the Console
motion.smoothie.getKey.title: Send config-get sd {key}
motion.smoothie.notice: Smoothieware keeps settings in its `config` file on the SD card — not GRBL `$`-settings. Read a value with `config-get sd <key>` and change one with `config-set sd <key> <value>` (a reset applies it).
motion.smoothie.ref.heading: Common config keys
motion.sync.label: ⟳ Sync
motion.sync.title: Sync — fetch all parameters from the machine ($$)
notif.label: Notifications
pcb.advanced.generateHint: Sends the active stage to the program store (same as a layer ▶, without streaming).
pcb.confirm.run:
RUN {verb} for "{name}" on the machine now?
{moves} moves, {mm} mm of cutting.
Make sure the work is clamped and Z is zeroed.
pcb.error.assignCopper: Assign a Copper Top/Bottom layer (or load a Gerber) for isolation routing.
pcb.error.assignDrill: Assign a Drill layer (or load an Excellon file) for drilling.
pcb.error.assignOutline: Assign a Board Outline or Copper layer to derive the cutout outline.
pcb.error.emptyBounds: Layer bounds are empty; cannot derive cutout outline.
pcb.error.noOperation: This layer role has no machining operation.
pcb.error.noToolpath: No toolpath produced for this stage.
pcb.essentials.hint: These apply to every operation. Fine-tune passes, depths and feeds under Advanced.
pcb.essentials.title: 3 · Essentials
pcb.intro.lead: Drop your board's Gerber/Excellon export ZIP here. Layers are detected automatically — then press
pcb.intro.rest: on a layer to run it.
pcb.layers.connectToRunTitle: Connect to the machine to run
pcb.layers.noOpTitle: No machining operation for this role
pcb.layers.title: 2 · Layers — press ▶ to run
pcb.layers.unrecognised_one: {count} file unrecognised — set a role below.
pcb.layers.unrecognised_other: {count} files unrecognised — set a role below.
pcb.legend.stream: stream to the machine. Adjust the cut in Advanced below.
pcb.load.applied: Loaded PCB settings — re-applied {n} layer roles.
pcb.load.bad: Could not load — not a valid PCB settings file.
pcb.load.paramsOnly: Loaded PCB settings. Upload a Gerber ZIP to apply the saved layer roles.
pcb.output.title: Output
pcb.status.connectFirst: Connect to the machine before running a layer.
pcb.status.preview: Preview {verb} for {name}: {moves} moves, cut {mm} mm. Shown in Visualizer.

pnp.field.placeDwell.title: Pause after releasing so the part settles (0 = none)
pnp.field.placeZ.title: Height the head lowers to when placing the part down
pnp.field.strength.title: Spindle S value = vacuum / grip strength (M3 S…)
pnp.field.travelZ.title: Safe clearance height for all XY travel

pnp.head.select.title: What is mounted at the head
pnp.hint.post: only. Acceleration is a global machine setting ($120–$122, set in the Motion / Probe panels) and is not written here.
pnp.intro.head: point. The head grabs with the spindle output ({on} on, {off} off). Build the operations below, then
pnp.load.bad: Could not load — not a valid pick & place file.
pnp.motion.title: Motion & {action}
pnp.ops.empty: No operations yet. Add one below, or set pick/place from the machine position.

pnp.preview.title: Bed preview · pick ○ → place △
pnp.rotaryAxis: Emit part rotation as a real A-axis word (G0 A…)
pnp.send.btn.title.off: Connect to a machine to send
pnp.send.btn.title.on: Stream this program to the machine
pnp.send.notConnected: Not connected — preview is live; connect to send.
pnp.send.title: Generate & send
pnp.setPick.title.on: Fill the selected op pick X/Y from the live machine position
pnp.setPlace.title.on: Fill the selected op place X/Y from the live machine position
pr.detect.alarm: Alarm during homing — check switches, then Unlock ($X) and retry.
pr.detect.autoTip: $H homing cycle, then read $130–$132 into the bed size. Moves the machine.
pr.detect.confirmNoTip: Cancel — do not move the machine
pr.detect.confirmQ: This will home the machine — keep clear. Continue?
pr.detect.confirmYesTip: Send $H and learn the workspace size
pr.detect.hint: Home the machine on its limit switches, then learn the work-area size from GRBL’s configured max travel. This drives the 3D bed grid and bed-fit checks.
pr.detect.homing: Homing… keep clear of the machine.
pr.detect.needHoming: Auto-home needs homing enabled. Open Motion / GRBL settings (⚙) and set $22=1 (homing) and wire your limit switches first.
pr.detect.noSettings: GRBL settings not read yet — press Sync, then retry.
pr.detect.noTravel: No usable max travel ($130–$132). Set them in Motion settings first.
pr.detect.noTravelAfter: Homed, but no usable max travel ($130–$132) to learn. Set them in Motion settings.
pr.detect.provenance: From GRBL’s configured max travel ($130–$132) — accurate only if those are calibrated to the real machine.
pr.detect.skipped: Skipped {axes} — set to 0 or unknown. Calibrate max travel in Motion settings.
pr.detect.title: Auto-detect workspace
pr.detect.useTravelTip: Copy the configured max travel ($130–$132) into the bed size. Does NOT move the machine.
print.arrange.hint: Object is auto-centred on the bed. Rotation is in 90° steps about Z.
print.arrange.title: 2 · Arrange
print.confirm:
Start the print on the machine now?
{lines} lines. Make sure the bed is clear and the printer is homed-safe.
print.err.noTriangles: STL parsed but contained no triangles.
print.gcode.label: G-code — {name}
print.import.title: 1 · Import STL
print.intro.post: arrange it on the bed, slice to G-code, then preview & stream it to a GRBL printer.
print.note.post: (inset perimeters + alternating 0/90° rectilinear infill) for hobby GRBL-based printers — not a production slicer (no supports, bridging, or adaptive layers). Always sanity-check the toolpath in the Visualizer before printing.
print.reset.title: Reset scale/rotation, auto-centre
print.rotate.title: Rotate 90° about Z
print.scaleFit.title: Scale down to fit the bed
print.settings.title: 3 · Print settings
print.slice.title: 4 · Slice & print
print.status.sliced: Sliced {layers} layers → {lines} lines{warn}. Shown in Visualizer & Program.
probe.adv.caution: Caution: hard limits ($21) need limit switches physically wired. Many switches are normally-closed — if a switch reads triggered while open in the lights above, turn ON limit-pins invert ($5). Soft limits ($20) only work after a successful homing cycle.
probe.adv.hardLimitsDesc: stop on a limit switch trigger (needs switches wired)
probe.adv.limitInvertDesc: invert limit inputs — set ON for NC (normally-closed) switches
probe.adv.softLimitsDesc: refuse moves past $130–$132 max travel (needs homing)
probe.adv.syncTip: $$ — re-read settings so the toggles reflect the machine
probe.adv.title: Advanced: limits & homing
probe.intro: Find Z=0 with a touch probe, and watch your limit / probe switches live. Advanced GRBL limit & homing settings are tucked below.
probe.switch.connectNote: Connect to a GRBL device to see live pin states.
probe.switch.hint: Press a switch by hand — it lights up here.
probe.switch.title: Live switch detection
probe.z.lastProbeTip: $# — dump coordinate systems incl. PRB (last probe result) to the console
probe.z.lastSent: Last probe sent — check the console / "Show last probe".
probe.z.maxDistSub: give up if no contact within this far
probe.z.noAlarmTip: G38.3 Z- F — probe toward the workpiece, but do NOT alarm if no contact is made.
probe.z.plateSub: thickness of the probe / touch plate
probe.z.probeTip: G38.2 Z- F — lower the tool until it touches the plate. Alarms if no contact within the max distance.
probe.z.safety1: lowers the tool — clip the probe clip to the tool and rest the plate on the workpiece first. 1) Place the plate. 2)
probe.z.safety5: so Z=0 sits at the plate surface.
probe.z.setZeroTip: G10 L20 P0 Z<thickness> — set work Z=0 at the plate surface. Run after a successful probe.
probe.z.speedSub: how fast to lower toward the workpiece
probe.z.title: Z-Probe
prog.editorHint: Edit G-code; changes apply when you click away
prog.editorPlaceholder: No program loaded. Drag & drop or ⬆ a .nc / .gcode file, or type / paste G-code here.
prog.empty: No program loaded. Load a .nc / .gcode file to begin.
prog.resetStartLine: Reset start line to 1 (restart from the top)
prog.startLineHint: Start line: Stream begins from this line (1-based)
prog.streamHint: Start streaming the program from the start line
sig.draw.placeholder: Sign here with mouse / stylus / touch
sig.draw.title: Sign here
sig.drop.hint: Click to choose an image, or drag & drop here
sig.drop.name: {name} — {w}×{h}px
sig.info.draw: Sign in the box above with your mouse, stylus, or finger.
sig.info.loadInvalid: Could not load — file is not a valid signature document.
sig.info.loaded: Loaded "{name}" ({w}×{h}px). Adjust threshold and size below.
sig.info.noInk: No ink detected — try adjusting the threshold or toggling Invert.
sig.info.start: Upload a signature image to begin.
sig.intro.draw: Sign freehand with your mouse, stylus, or finger — your strokes become pen-plotter G-code live. Each pen-down stroke plots in order, with a safe-Z hop between strokes.
sig.intro.post: (the contour of each stroke), not its centreline.
sig.intro.pre: Upload a signature image — it is traced to vector outlines live, then sent as pen-plotter G-code. Best with a clean, high-contrast signature on white. This traces ink
sig.preview.noVectors: No vectors yet — adjust the threshold.
sig.preview.title: Traced preview
sig.preview.upload: Upload an image to see the live trace here.
sig.regen.title: Regenerate now
sig.send.live: Preview is live; connect a machine to send.
sig.send.title.off: Connect to a machine to send
sig.send.title.on: Stream this program to the machine
solder.defaults.body: Values used to prefill each newly added point. Free-Z is the travel height; Touch-Z is where the tip touches down.
solder.defaults.title: New-point defaults
solder.feeder.body: Safe-Z retract height, feeder spindle speed (S), plunge feed rate, and the settle dwell after each touch-down.
solder.feeder.title: Feeder & motion
solder.field.decimals.body: Number of decimal places in the emitted coordinates.
solder.field.feed.body: How long the wire feeder runs at each point (seconds).
solder.field.feedType.body: Pre-solder feeds wire before touch-down; touch-down feeds while the tip is down.
solder.field.feederS.body: Spindle speed word (S) that drives the solder-wire feeder.
solder.field.freeZ.body: Travel height the tip lifts to between points.
solder.field.plungeF.body: Feed rate used to lower the tip from Free-Z to Touch-Z.
solder.field.safeZ.body: Guaranteed retract height before any XY travel and at program end.
solder.field.settle.body: Dwell after each touch-down so the joint settles before lifting.
solder.field.touchZ.body: Depth the tip touches down to at each point.
solder.intro: Solders a list of points one by one. The spindle output drives a solder-wire feeder (M3 runs it, M5 stops). The program auto-syncs to the Program tab for streaming.
solder.intro.bold: spindle output drives a solder-wire feeder
solder.intro.lead: Solders a list of points one by one. The machine's
solder.intro.rest: (M3 runs it, M5 stops). Add points or record positions, tune the feeder, then send.
solder.live.title: Lines auto-synced to the Program tab
solder.notConnected: Not connected — preview is live; connect from the Program tab to stream.
solder.points.hint: New points are prefilled from the defaults below. Click a row to select it (then "Record position" fills its X/Y).
solder.points.title: Solder points
solder.send.notConnected: Not connected — preview is live; connect to send.
solder.send.play.title.connect: Connect to a machine to send
solder.send.play.title.send: Stream this program to the machine
solder.table.empty: No points yet. Press + to add one, or ⌖ to record the machine position.
solder.title: Auto-solder
solder.toolbar.add.body: Append a soldering point prefilled from the defaults in Settings.
solder.toolbar.add.title: Add a soldering point prefilled from the defaults below
solder.toolbar.clear.body: Remove every soldering point and start over.
solder.toolbar.clear.title: Remove all soldering points
solder.toolbar.loadCsv.body: Load a solder-point list from a CSV file on your PC (replaces the current list).
solder.toolbar.record.body.append: Appends a point at the current machine position.
solder.toolbar.record.body.connect: Connect to a machine to capture its live position.
solder.toolbar.record.body.fill: Fills the selected row X/Y from the live machine position.
solder.toolbar.record.title.append: Append a point at the current machine position
solder.toolbar.record.title.connect: Connect to record the machine position
solder.toolbar.record.title.fill: Fill selected row X/Y from machine position
solder.toolbar.saveCsv.body: Download the current solder-point list as a CSV file you can re-load later.
solder.toolbar.settings.body: New-point defaults plus feeder and motion parameters (Safe-Z, feeder S, plunge feed, dwell).
vp.frame.feed.title: Feed rate for the perimeter trace (mm/min)
vp.frame.inset.title: Inset (+) shrinks the trace inward; margin (−) grows it outward, per side (mm)
vp.frame.loops.title: How many times to loop the perimeter
vp.frame.tip.noBounds: Frame — generate a program with XY bounds first.
vp.frame.tip.notIdle: Frame — machine is {state}; wait until it is Idle.
vp.frame.tip.ready: Frame — trace the job perimeter ({w}×{h} mm) at safe Z with the tool OFF, so you can check placement.
vp.frame.tip.readyNoSize: Frame — trace the job perimeter with the tool OFF.
vp.frame.title: Frame
vp.menu.title: Add shape
vz.bedSize.title: Bed size (work area)
vz.cameraOverlay: Show live camera 3D (bed + job from the Camera panel)
vz.carveSim: Material removal simulation (carve the stock as it runs)
vz.fit.title: Whether the program fits within the machine work area (bed {bed} mm)
vz.footprint.title: Footprint area covered by the toolpath
vz.size.title: Width (X) × Depth (Y) of the loaded program's bounding box, in mm
vz.zrange.title: Z range (top→bottom) and total cut depth, in mm
weld.arc.body: The spindle output is the welder/arc: M3 (S = power) strikes it, M5 stops it. Gas pre-flow runs before striking; post-flow runs after stopping.
weld.arc.title: Arc & gas
weld.empty: No objects yet. Press ╱ for a 3D line or ◯ for a circle, then ⇤ / ⇥ / ⊙ to record machine positions.
weld.field.amplitude.body: Transverse half-width of the weave (mm). Bead width ≈ 2× this.
weld.field.arcPower.body: Emitted as the S word (M3 S<power>). Set 0 for a plain M3 with no power word.
weld.field.decimals.body: Number of decimal places in the emitted coordinates.
weld.field.pattern.body: Straight = no weave; Zigzag = triangular; Sine = smooth; Circular = forward loops.
weld.field.pitch.body: Forward travel per one full weave cycle (mm/cycle). Smaller = a tighter, faster-oscillating weave; cycles-per-mm = 1/pitch.
weld.field.plungeF.body: Feed rate used to lower the torch from Safe-Z to the weld Z.
weld.field.postFlow.body: Gas dwell (G4 P) after the arc stops, before retract. 0 = none.
weld.field.preFlow.body: Gas dwell (G4 P) after lowering but before the arc strikes. 0 = none.
weld.field.safeZ.body: Guaranteed retract height before any XY travel and at program end.
weld.field.segs.body: Sampled points per weave cycle. Higher = smoother sine/circular curves at the cost of more G-code lines.
weld.field.useArc.body: Emit M3/M5 around each seam. Turn off to move the path without striking the arc (dry run).
weld.field.weldFeed.body: Travel speed of the torch along the seam (mm/min).
weld.intro: Welds one or more straight seams with a transverse weave. The spindle output drives the welder/arc (M3 on, M5 off) with gas pre/post-flow dwells. The program auto-syncs to the Program tab for streaming.
weld.live.title: Lines auto-synced to the Program tab
weld.load.bad: Could not load — not a valid welding file.
weld.motion.body: The weld feed is how fast the torch travels along the seam. Safe-Z is the guaranteed retract; plunge feed lowers to the weld Z.
weld.motion.title: Travel & motion
weld.notConnected: Not connected — preview is live; connect from the Program tab to stream.
weld.objects.title: Weld objects
weld.seams.title: Weld seams
weld.table.empty: No seams yet. Press + to add one, then ⇤ / ⇥ to record machine positions.
weld.title: Welding
weld.toolbar.add.body: Append a straight weld seam you can edit (start, end, weld Z).
weld.toolbar.addCircle.body: Append a circular weld (centre {x,y,z} + radius), perimeter traced in the XY plane.
weld.toolbar.addLine.body: Append a 3D weld line (start {x,y,z} → end {x,y,z}) you can edit.
weld.toolbar.clear.body: Remove every seam and start over.
weld.toolbar.connect: Connect to a machine to capture its live position.
weld.toolbar.recCenter.body: Set the selected circle CENTRE {x,y,z} from the live machine position.
weld.toolbar.recEnd.body: Set the selected seam END from the live machine position.
weld.toolbar.recStart.body: Set the selected seam START (and weld Z) from the live machine position.
weld.toolbar.settings.body: Weave pattern + amplitude/pitch, weld & plunge feeds, arc power, and gas pre/post-flow.
weld.weave.body: A transverse oscillation superimposed perpendicular to the seam to widen the bead. Amplitude is the half-width; pitch is the forward travel per full cycle (smaller pitch = faster, tighter weave).
weld.weave.patSpeed.body: A second, independent speed (mm/min) that sets how fast the weave oscillates. Density = pattern-speed ÷ travel-speed: higher pattern speed packs more cycles per mm (denser weave), lower stretches them out (scattered).
weld.weave.title: Weave pattern
writing.alignment.tip: Horizontal alignment of each line of text.
writing.charHeight.tip: Cap height of the text in millimetres (font size).
writing.feed.tip: Feed — drawing (pen-down) feed rate, in mm per minute.
writing.font.builtinTip: Use the built-in Hershey single-stroke font (always available, works offline).
writing.font.loadTip: Load a custom single-stroke font JSON (from the handwriting pipeline).
writing.font.pickTip: Choose a font: built-in Hershey (offline), a bundled font, an uploaded file, or one of your loaded system fonts.
writing.font.systemNa: System fonts need a Chromium browser (Chrome/Edge) over HTTPS or localhost.
writing.font.systemTip: Load all fonts installed on this computer (asks for the browser "Fonts" permission).
writing.font.title: Font
writing.font.uploadTip: Upload a custom font: single-stroke JSON, or a TrueType/OpenType .ttf/.otf for outline mode.
writing.info.loadInvalid: Could not load — file is not a valid writing document.
writing.info.loadingSystem: Requesting access to your system fonts…
writing.info.localStale: Reload system fonts to use this font.
writing.intro: Type text → it previews live in the Visualizer and auto-syncs to the Program tab for streaming. Choose a font, style it, and pick Stroke (centerlines) or Outline (glyph contours) — output is safe pen-plotter G-code (Z = pen up / down).
writing.letterSpacing.tip: Extra gap added after each character, in millimetres.
writing.lineSpacing.tip: Baseline-to-baseline distance as a multiple of char height.
writing.missingGlyphs: Missing glyph(s): {glyphs} — rendered as blank space.
writing.mode.label: G-code mode
writing.mode.outlineNa: Outline mode needs a TTF/OTF font. Pick or upload one.
writing.mode.outlineTip: Engrave around each glyph contour. Best for TTF/OTF fonts.
writing.mode.strokeTip: Follow the font centerlines (single-stroke). Best for Hershey/JSON fonts; outline fonts use the built-in stroke font.
writing.originX.tip: Shift the whole text block along X, in millimetres.
writing.originY.tip: Shift the whole text block along Y, in millimetres.
writing.penDownZ.tip: Pen-down Z — height the pen drops to while drawing, in mm.
writing.penLayout.title: Pen & Layout
writing.penUpZ.tip: Pen-up Z — height the pen lifts to for travel moves (safe-Z), in mm.
writing.raw.placeholder: Generated G-code preview will appear here.
writing.raw.title: Raw G-code
writing.send.connectTip: Connect to a machine to send
writing.send.previewLive: Preview is live; connect to a machine to send.
writing.send.streamTip: Stream this program to the machine
writing.send.title: Send
writing.style.bold: Bold — thicken strokes with extra parallel passes.
writing.style.label: Text style
writing.style.underline: Underline — add a line under each row of text.
writing.text.placeholder: Type here. Use Enter for a new line.
writing.text.title: Text
writing.text.title.tip: Text to plot. Press Enter for a new line.
